Compute interpolation weights for Weighted Coefficient Kriging (WCK)

Module Contents

wdegp_utils.differences_by_dim_func(X1, X2, n_order, index=-1)[source]

Computes dimension-wise differences, applying a single hypercomplex perturbation if either the row (i) or column (j) index is selected.

This optimized version avoids broadcasting and boolean mask indexing to work correctly with OTI arrays.

Parameters:

  • X1 (ndarray of shape (n1, d)) – First input array.

  • X2 (ndarray of shape (n2, d)) – Second input array.

  • n_order (int) – Derivative order for the hypercomplex type.

  • index (list or int, optional) – Indices where derivative information is present. Default is -1 (no tagging).

Returns:

differences_by_dim – List of length d. Each entry is an (n1, n2) array of differences.

Return type:

list of oti.array

wdegp_utils.deriv_map(nbases, order)[source]

Creates a mapping from (order, index_within_order) to a single flattened index for all derivative components.

wdegp_utils.transform_der_indices(der_indices, der_map)[source]

Transforms a list of user-facing derivative specifications into the internal (order, index) format and the final flattened index.

wdegp_utils.rbf_kernel(differences, length_scales, n_order, n_bases, kernel_func, der_indices, powers, index=-1)[source]

Constructs the RBF kernel matrix with derivative entries using an efficient pre-allocation strategy combined with a single call to extract all derivative components.

Parameters:

function) ((Parameters are the same as the original)

Returns:

K – Full RBF kernel matrix with mixed function and derivative entries.

Return type:

ndarray

wdegp_utils.determine_weights(diffs_by_dim, diffs_test, length_scales, kernel_func, sigma_n)[source]

Compute interpolation weights for Weighted Coefficient Kriging (WCK) using a radial basis function (RBF) kernel.

This method constructs a modified system to solve for interpolation weights that minimize the kriging variance, including a Lagrange multiplier to enforce unbiasedness.

Parameters:

  • diffs_by_dim (list of ndarray) – Pairwise differences between training points (by dimension).

  • diffs_test (list of ndarray) – Pairwise differences between training and test point (by dimension).

  • length_scales (array-like) – Kernel hyperparameters (log-scaled or raw depending on kernel_func).

  • kernel_func (callable) – Kernel function (e.g., squared exponential, Matérn) accepting differences and returning a scalar covariance matrix.

Returns:

w – Interpolation weights for combining weighted Taylor basis coefficients in the kriging predictor.

Return type:

ndarray of shape (n_train,)

Notes

This method solves the augmented kriging system:

[ K F ] [w ] = [r] [ F^T 0 ] [μ ] [1]

where:

  • K is the covariance matrix of the training basis coefficients,

  • r is the covariance vector between the test point and training coefficients,

  • F is a column vector of ones enforcing unbiasedness,

  • μ is the Lagrange multiplier,

  • w contains the interpolation weights used in Weighted Coefficient Kriging.
